dialogos.texto_en: (What's going on?\n Both of them are looking around nervously...)
Yo, is something wrong?
Oh, it's Uesugi-kun.
Don't scare me like that.
I just called out to you...\nDid something happen?
Well, we were just looking around\nthe Native Okinawan Village when%p-2;――
Someone from the tour guide staff told us that \nthere's a yokai monster called 'Kijimuna' in Okinawa.
(Huh? Are they scared of yokai?\n Itsuki, for sure, is easily frightened, but...)
I can't help but think that there might be\na Kijimuna around here too...
Hey-hey, there's nothing to be this scared of.
B-but Kijimuna seemed like a good yokai, didn't it?
Are there really nice yokai out there?
Yes, Kijimuna is actually friendly with humans!\nIt's good at catching fish, so if you\ngo fishing together, you'll always catch a lot!
So, if you can become friends with Kijimuna,\nyou'll usually become rich!
Wow, it really does seem like a good yokai.\nI'd sure like to get to know it, wouldn't you?
Well, there's still more to this story.
More?
Although Kijimuna seems like a good yokai\nat first glance%p-2;――
It's said that if someone deceives or betrays Kijimuna,\nit will curse them completely\nand exact a terrible revenge.
P-please don't remind me of that!
What kind of terrible revenge are we talking about?
It's scary just to say it out loud...
Well, that's just a typical yokai story!\nJust don't do anything to provoke it,\nand you'll be fine!
But isn't it possible that humans and yokai\nhave different values and perspectives?
What if we've unwittingly\nangered Kijimuna without realizing it...!
Yokai are just made up stories.\nDon't take fiction so seriously.
...That's not the problem though, is it?
It seems that our values\nand those of Uesugi-kun also differ.
Even among humans, we can have different \nvalues that make it seem more difficult to \nunderstand one another.
Even for us quintuplets,\nthere are many things that don't work out.
...That's right.
It's the gods you don't want to anger.
If you ever see an actual yokai, \nmaybe pretending you didn't \nwould be best for both your sakes.
...That might be the best course of action.
But if you do actually meet one,\nthere might be no turning back, you know?
Even among humans, we can have different \nvalues that make it seem more difficult to \nunderstand one another.
Even for us quintuplets,\nthere are many things that don't work out.
...That's right.
And how do you know that humans and yokai\nhave different values?
Huh?\nWell...
If it's a yokai that lives close to humans,\nit's likely that they would already know about\nthe human way of life, as well.
Or, is it a fundamental difference in worldview?\nIf that's the case, it's possible for both parties\nto have different values for the same object, isn't it.
Well, you don't have to think about it that deeply.
Fufu! But that's so Uesugi-kun.
Even among humans, we can have different \nvalues that make it seem more difficult to \nunderstand one another.
Even for us quintuplets,\nthere are many things that don't work out.
...That's right.
(They look so alike when you look at them \n like this but they are definitely different people.)
(It's precisely these differences \n that they want me to figure out.)
(With that meaning in mind, maybe \n that's why they gave me this trial...)
